sections.paragraph:
ἡγεμών, όνος, ὁ (Hom.+; loanw. in rabb.).

① one who rules, esp. in a preeminent position, ruler (Soph., Oed. R. 103; SIG 814, 25; Ex 15:15; Job 42:17d; Jos., Ant. 19, 217. Perh. ‘chieftain’ Gen 36:15ff; 1 Ch 1:51ff) ἐν τοῖς ἡγεμόσιν Ἰούδα among the rulers of Judah Mt 2:6 (after Mi 5:1; the rendering ἐν τ. ἡγεμόσιν instead of the LXX ἐν χιλιάσιν, following rabbinic methods of interpretation, is suggested by ἡγούμενον in 2 Km 5:2, cited in the last part of Mt 2:6. On the view that a misunderstanding of the original text or a variant in the LXX tradition underlies the text, s. comm.).

② head imperial provincial administrator, governor in the provinces (Dio Chrys. 31 [48], 1; Ael. Aristid. 50, 12 K.= 26 p. 505 D.; OGI index; pap; Jos., Ant. 15, 405) Mt 10:18; 27:23 v.l.; Mk 13:9; Lk 21:12; 1 Pt 2:14. Esp. of the procurators or prefects in Judaea: Pontius Pilate (Jos., Ant. 18, 55 Πιλᾶτος δὲ ὁ τ. Ἰουδαίας ἡγεμών; JVardaman, A New Inscr. [Lat.] which Mentions Pilate as ‘Prefect’, JBL 81, ’62, 70f; Boffo, Iscrizione no. 25) Mt 27:2, 11, 14 f, 21, 27; 28:14; Lk 20:20; Felix Ac 23:24, 26, 33; 24:1, 10; Festus 26:30.—Jerome (in Epheus) AcPl Ha 1, 23. WLiebenam, Beiträge z. Verwaltungsgesch. d. röm. Reiches I 1886, 1ff; Pauly-W. XXIII, 1, 1240–79. GBurton, Provincial Procurators and the Public Provinces: Chiron 23, ’93, 13–28.—LRobert, AntCl 62, ’60, 329. DELG s.v. ἡγέομαι. EDNT. M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

esp. esp. = especially

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ff ff = (and) following

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

comm. comm. = commentary, -aries, commentator(s)

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ael. Aristid. Ael. Aristid. = Aelius Aristides, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Lat. Lat. = Latin

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 433
